description: The market capitalization of the company, typically float-adjusted; represents total equity market value
Modeled elasticity: percentage change in intrinsic stock value (return) per 1% change in the discount rate, using the FLEIR (forward-looking DCF-based) exposure framework
Implied cost of equity (required rate of return) for the stock, estimated using a discounted cash flow or dividend discount model within the FLEIR framework
Modeled percentage change in intrinsic stock value (from DCF) when the discount rate is increased by 25 basis points, representing fundamental interest rate sensitivity
Beta of stock returns to oil price returns (specifically, West Texas Intermediate crude), representing sensitivity to oil as a macro factor
Score reflecting a company's fundamental pricing power, i.e., its ability to pass on inflation-induced cost increases to customers
Composite sensitivity blending purified breakeven inflation beta and interest rate sensitivity beta
Stock return sensitivity to "purified" changes in breakeven inflation rates, i.e. the portion of inflation risk orthogonal to changes in 10Y Treasury yields
Statistical beta measuring stock's sensitivity to raw changes in breakeven inflation rates (market-implied inflation expectations)
Stock's statistical beta (sensitivity) to parallel (level) shifts in the Treasury yield curve, i.e. how much stock returns move when the overall level of interest rates changes
Stock's statistical beta (sensitivity) to slope changes in the Treasury yield curve, i.e. sensitivity to changes in spread between short- and long-term rates
